task:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Fix the production API docs URL (was 404 at `/docs/`)
   - Fix "Switch to MCP API" and "Switch to Map API" cross-links pointing to `localhost:8765` instead of production URL
   - Fix Swagger code block text unreadable in light mode
   - Fix dark mode not applying to the white preamble header on `/map-api/`
   - Create a combined `/docs/` page with Map API and MCP API in tabs, styled like admin pages
   - Make the Safecast logo (image + single-color text) appear on all admin and API/docs pages
   - Unify the top-nav header across ALL admin pages and the `/docs/` page: sticky navy bar, logo, page title, "Back to Map", light/dark toggle
   - Fix the MCP API tab in the combined `/docs/` page ("Failed to load API definition" error)
   - Commit, push, PR, merge all changes on both GitHub and Codeberg, and document

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Go `net/http` ServeMux — routes registered on the default mux (port 8765) vs. a custom MCP mux (port 3333)
   - `swaggo/http-swagger` — two swagger instances: `"unifiedapi"` (Map API from `docs/api/`) and default `"swagger"` (MCP API from `docs/`)
   - nginx proxy routing — two configs: `origin-simplemap.safecast.org` and `simplemap.safecast.org` must both be updated
   - CloudFront caching — cached 404s require explicit invalidation via `aws cloudfront create-invalidation`
   - Admin CSS variable system: `--bg-primary`, `--bg-card`, `--text-primary`, `--border-color`, `--link-color`, etc.
   - Dark mode via `data-theme` attribute on `<html>`, stored in `localStorage` key `safecastDocTheme`
   - SwaggerUIBundle multiple instances on one page using `domNode` and `BaseLayout`
   - Lazy initialization of second swagger instance on first tab click
   - systemd service `Environment=` lines for production env vars

3. Files and Code Sections
Did you build the localhost server too?
user_feedback_text: Did you build the localhost server too?
